questions.question: If $f(x) = x \cdot 2$ and $g(x) = x + 3$, what is $(f+g)(x)$?
What is the domain of $(f/g)(x)$ if $f(x) = x^2$ and $g(x) = x - 4$?
The rhythm pattern of a song is defined by $f(x) = \sin(x)$ and $g(x) = \cos(x)$. What is $(f \cdot g)(x)$?
If $h(x) = (f/g)(x)$ and $f(x) = x + 2$ and $g(x) = x - 1$, what is $h(3)$?
What is the simplified expression of $(x^2 + 3) \cdot (x - 2)$?
If $f(x) = x^2$ and $g(x) = x - 1$, what is $(f - g)(x)$?
What is the domain of $(f+g)(x)$ if $f(x) = 1/x$ and $g(x) = 2/x$?
If $f(x) = x + 1$ and $g(x) = x - 2$, what is $(f/g)(x)$?

The canvas size of a painting is defined by $f(x) = x + 2$ and $g(x) = 2x - 1$. Determine the domain of $(f \cdot g)(x)$ and simplify the resulting expression. Show all steps and calculate the value of $(f \cdot g)(2)$.

The frequency of a sound wave is defined by $h(x) = (f/g)(x)$ where $f(x) = \sin(x)$ and $g(x) = \cos(x)$. Determine the domain of $h(x)$ and simplify the resulting expression. Show all steps and calculate the value of $h(\pi/4)$.

teacher_rules: Tip 1: Ensure students provide the correct units for their answers.
Tip 2: Require students to show all steps for free response questions.
Tip 3: Remind students to check their work for accuracy and completeness.
